name: devmemory-ai-vscode
description: Stop your AI from forgetting your codebase — and from suggesting `rm -rf`. Local memory and a destructive-paste guard for Claude, Copilot, Cursor, Cline, Continue, and on-prem LLMs. Includes @devmemory chat participant and MCP server.

commands.title: DevMemory AI: Add Manual Session Note
DevMemory AI: Teach The AI About This Project
DevMemory AI: Save What The AI Told Me
DevMemory AI: Save What The AI Did
DevMemory AI: Export Context To CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / Copilot / Cursor
DevMemory AI: View Paste Guard Log

devmemory.confirmBeforeScan.description: Ask for explicit confirmation before scanning allowed workspace files.

devmemory.pasteGuard.enabled.description: Inspect large pastes for destructive commands (rm -rf, drop database, fork bomb, …) and block before they hit your code.

devmemory.pasteGuard.minChars.description: Minimum number of characters in a single insertion before the paste guard inspects it.

devmemory.pasteGuard.allowedFileGlobs.description: Globs (relative to the workspace root) where the paste guard never runs (typically tests and docs).

devmemory.telemetry.enabled.description: Append local-only event lines to .ai-memory/telemetry/. Never sent over the network. Inspect with "View Local Telemetry" or clear with "Clear Local Telemetry".

devmemory.registerChatParticipant.description: Register @devmemory in Copilot Chat / VS Code Chat so any chat agent can reach memory without clipboard.

devmemory.exposeMcpServer.description: Run a local MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that exposes .ai-memory/ to Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Continue, and any MCP-aware client. Auto-writes .vscode/mcp.json.

devmemory.autoAttachContext.description: When the VS Code Chat / Copilot Chat opens, surface a one-click action to inject the current resume prompt as context.

chatParticipants.name: devmemory
chatParticipants.description: Local project memory for any chat agent. No telemetry. No network calls.

commands.name: resume
commands.description: Inject a fresh resume prompt with current project memory

commands.name: decisions
commands.description: Show decisions logged in this project

commands.name: save-session
commands.description: Append a session draft from the current chat

languageModelTools.name: devmemory_getProjectSummary
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the local DevMemory project-summary.md content for this workspace. Read-only. Use before answering questions about the project's purpose, tech stack, or scope.

languageModelTools.name: devmemory_getArchitecture
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the local DevMemory architecture.md content for this workspace. Read-only. Use before discussing modules, boundaries, or data flow.

languageModelTools.name: devmemory_getCurrentState
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns DevMemory current-state.md content. Read-only. Use to know what is working, in progress, or known-broken before suggesting changes.

languageModelTools.name: devmemory_searchDecisions
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Searches the local DevMemory decisions/decisions.md log for entries matching a keyword. Read-only. Use when you need to recall a past architectural choice.

query.description: Substring (case-insensitive) to search for in the decisions log.

languageModelTools.name: devmemory_auditAiResponse
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Validates an AI-produced text for destructive shell commands (rm -rf, DROP DATABASE, fork bombs, etc.) and simulated/fictitious content. Returns warnings before the user runs anything. Use this on any AI proposal that includes shell commands or claims about files.

walkthroughs.title: Get started with DevMemory AI
walkthroughs.description: Stop your AI from forgetting your codebase. Block destructive commands before you paste them. 4 quick steps.

steps.title: Scan this project (15 sec)
steps.description:
DevMemory builds a local Markdown summary of your repo with strict secret exclusions (~140 patterns). Nothing leaves your machine.
[Scan this project](command:devmemory.initializeProject)

steps.title: See the paste guard catch a destructive command
steps.description:
Open any file and paste an AI suggestion that contains `rm -rf`, `git push --force`, or `DROP DATABASE`. DevMemory blocks the paste modally with one-click Undo.
[Open the paste guard log](command:devmemory.viewPasteGuardLog)

steps.title: Resume an AI session in 1 click
steps.description:
DevMemory builds a fresh resume prompt with the current project memory. Use the @devmemory chat participant inside Copilot Chat, or copy-paste into Claude Code / Cursor / Cline / Continue.
[Resume an AI session](command:devmemory.generateResumePrompt)

steps.title: Connect Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, or Continue
steps.description:
DevMemory writes a managed block into CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, and .github/copilot-instructions.md so other AI tools auto-read your memory. Anything outside the markers is preserved.
[Export AI context files](command:devmemory.exportAIContextFiles)

scripts.bundle: npm run bundle:extension && npm run bundle:mcp
scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run build && npm run bundle
